title: Grove App Manifest v0.3
description: On-disk manifest format for a Grove app. One file per app at apps/<owner>/<name>/manifest.json. Compose-shape: app declares one or more services that run together; grove resolves to a containerd runtime (local via dew, cloud via creekd).

schema_version.description: Major.minor format. Both "0.3" and "0.3.<patch>" validate against this schema — patch versions are reserved for additive, backward-compatible field additions that don't force re-tagging existing manifests. A bump to 0.4 indicates a breaking change and requires migration. grove CLI checks the major.minor against what its bundled validator supports.

slug.description: <owner>/<name>. Lowercase. Matches the directory path apps/<slug>/manifest.json. The slug is used directly as a filesystem path component. Owner is dot-separated alphanumeric segments (each segment starts+ends with [a-z0-9], may contain dashes) — this structure naturally forbids '..', leading/trailing dots, and consecutive dots without needing regex lookahead (so the same pattern compiles under both RE2 and PCRE). Apps published outside github.com prefix owner with the forge hostname (e.g. codeberg.org/forgejo/forgejo).

name.description: Display name shown in grove UI.

version.description: Upstream version this manifest tracks. SemVer preferred but free-form (some upstreams use date tags).

description.description: One-line tagline (<= 200 chars). Shown in list views.

upstream.description: Canonical project URL (usually the source repo).

license.description: SPDX license identifier of the app (e.g. "Apache-2.0", "AGPL-3.0").

tags.description: Search / category tags. Lowercase. Internal characters may include '-' and '.' (e.g. 'next.js', 'pocket-alternative'); must start and end with alphanumeric. Single characters are allowed.

source.description: Provenance of the primary app image. See $defs/source.

arch.description: CPU architectures this app supports. All images in services[] must be available for every arch listed here.

secrets.description: Secrets generated by grove at install time, stored in per-app state. Services reference them via ${secret.NAME} interpolation inside env values (see service.env). Generated once per install; persisted; included in deploy snapshot.

user_inputs.description: Values the user must supply at install time (SMTP server, OIDC client, admin email, custom domain, license key). Grove prompts during `grove install` and stores answers in per-app state. Services reference them via ${user.NAME} interpolation in env values. Distinct from secrets: user_inputs are knowable to the user (they typed them), secrets are generated bytes the user never sees. NOT included in deploy snapshot — the cloud-side install re-prompts (typical: same SMTP creds reused, new domain entered). Optional; many apps have none.

services.description: One or more containers that together compose this app. Services share a single private network; reach each other by service name as DNS. At least one service must have a port with public: true.

image_digest.description: Digest pin of the upstream image. REQUIRED for source.type=upstream — tag pinning (v3.2.1, 16-alpine) is not sufficient because tags can be re-pushed silently. Grove tooling (grove pin) auto-fetches the current digest for a tag.

oneOf.description: Image published by upstream project or a known third-party packager. Grove just points at it, pinned by digest for supply-chain safety.

image_digest.description: Digest of the mirrored image as pushed to ghcr.io/solcreek.

upstream_digest.description: Digest of the source image at the moment of mirroring. Lets a reviewer verify the mirror is bit-identical to upstream at that point in time.

oneOf.description: Grove team mirrored an upstream image to ghcr.io/solcreek for availability + digest pinning. No code change.
Grove team built this image from source (often because upstream doesn't publish one, or because we patched something). Build is reproducible from repo + ref.

name.description: Identifier used by services to reference this input via ${user.NAME} in env values. UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.

label.description: Short human label shown in the install prompt (e.g. 'Admin email').

description.description: Optional longer explanation shown beneath the label (1-2 sentences). Use to clarify format or where to find the value.

type.description: Input type. Grove UI / CLI render accordingly: 'password' is masked; 'multiline' opens a textarea; 'email' / 'url' / 'integer' add format validation. 'boolean' renders as a checkbox.

required.description: When true (default), grove refuses to start the app until the user provides a value. When false, the field is optional and the input may be omitted entirely (services referencing ${user.NAME} for an empty optional input get an empty string).

default.description: Pre-filled value shown in the prompt. Type must match the input's type field — string for string/email/url/multiline/password, integer for integer, boolean for boolean. Omit if there's no sensible default.

placeholder.description: Example value shown in the input field but NOT submitted (typical UI placeholder behavior). Use to demonstrate format without imposing a default.

name.description: Identifier used by services to reference this secret. UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.

generate.description: Generation rule. Currently only "random:N" where N is byte length (16/24/32/48/64).

format.description: Encoding of the generated bytes. Default is alphanumeric — URL-safe, shell-safe, and works inside connection strings like postgres://user:PASS@host. Pick hex if you need slightly higher entropy density at the same character class. Pick base64 ONLY when the consumer explicitly requires base64 (it contains '+', '/', '=' which break URLs and most shell-escaping).

fork_policy.description: What happens to this secret when grove forks the app to the cloud (Deploy button). 'preserve' (default, safer): the cloud copy reuses the local secret value verbatim — required for secrets that sign existing data (cookie signing keys, TOTP vault keys, encryption-at-rest keys) where regeneration would silently invalidate the data carried over in the snapshot. 'rotate': the cloud copy generates a fresh value — appropriate for secrets that are coupled to ephemeral state grove can update simultaneously on both sides (e.g. the POSTGRES_PASSWORD when both the postgres service and the app service env get updated together). When unsure, 'preserve' is the correct default — getting it wrong by rotating something that should preserve bricks the user's data; getting it wrong by preserving something that could rotate is merely suboptimal.

name.description: DNS-safe name. Used as hostname between services in the app's private network. Must be unique within services[].

image_digest.description: Optional digest pin. When present, runtime verifies image matches before starting.

command.description: Override the image's ENTRYPOINT. Rare — most services use the image default. NO ${secret.NAME} interpolation here — secrets are only resolved in env values; reference them through env vars and read those in the entrypoint.

args.description: Override the image's CMD. Common for tuning flags (e.g. seaweedfs needs --filer.port). NO ${secret.NAME} interpolation here — secrets are only resolved in env values; if a flag needs a secret, read it from an env var inside the container's entrypoint.

env.description:
Literal env vars. Values may contain three interpolation forms:

  ${secret.NAME} — injects a secret declared in top-level secrets[]. Unknown name = validation error.

  ${user.NAME} — injects a user-supplied input declared in top-level user_inputs[]. Unknown name = validation error. Empty-but-optional input substitutes the empty string.

  ${grove.public_url} — the externally-reachable URL of this app instance (local: http://localhost:<host_port>; cloud: https://<assigned domain>). Always available.

All three share the same grammar: <NAME> matches ^[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*$; longest-match on the closing '}'; to emit a literal '${' write '$${' (the runtime collapses '$${' → '${' after interpolation); unknown variable names are validation errors, never silent empty substitution. NO other interpolation forms are supported (no service introspection, no expression evaluation, no env-var-from-env-var). ${grove.old_public_url} is NOT available here — only inside hooks.post_restore.cmd.

volumes.description: Persistent or ephemeral mounts inside this container.

ports.description: Ports this service listens on. Set public: true on the port(s) the user reaches externally. Inter-service ports do not need to be listed — services can reach each other on any port via the private network.

health.description: Health probe. Used by depends_on resolution and by the reverse proxy to gate traffic.

depends_on.description: Names of other services in this manifest that must reach "healthy" state before this service starts. Forms a DAG; cycles rejected at validation.

hooks.description: Lifecycle hooks that run inside this service's container at specific points. Use post_restore to rewrite data after a fork-deploy lands (URL search-replace for apps that persist absolute URLs in DB rows — Ghost, Plausible, Nextcloud). Use pre_upgrade / post_upgrade to drain queues, run migrations, etc.

restart_policy.description: What grove does when this container exits. 'always' (default): restart unconditionally — right for long-running services (web, db, queue worker). 'on-failure': restart only on non-zero exit — for jobs that legitimately finish. 'no': never restart — for one-shot bootstrap containers. Grove's own stop / down commands always succeed regardless of policy.

memory_mb.description: Hard memory ceiling for this container. OOM-kill kicks in above this — runtime sets cgroup memory.limit_in_bytes. Skip when in doubt; grove falls back to a generous per-profile default.

cpu_shares.description: Relative CPU weight under contention. 1024 = baseline; 2048 = twice the share; 512 = half. Has no effect when CPU is idle. Use to mark a service as lower-priority than its peers (e.g. background indexer vs web frontend).

resources.description: Resource ceilings. Both fields optional — omitting lets grove use defaults. Set memory_mb when an app is known to leak (some Node services); set cpu_shares when one service should yield to another under contention.

stop_timeout_seconds.description: Grace period after SIGTERM before grove sends SIGKILL during shutdown. Default 10s matches Docker's. Raise for databases that need long graceful shutdown (Postgres ~30s, ClickHouse ~60s); lower for stateless services to free resources faster on stop.

name.description: Identifier within this app's namespace. Used by grove to name the persistent volume on disk. Underscores allowed (volume names don't need to be DNS-safe).

path.description: Absolute path inside the container where the volume mounts.

role.description: What this volume IS, which determines its lifecycle and whether it's snapshotted at fork-deploy time. 'state' (default): authoritative data that survives restart AND must be included in deploy snapshots (e.g. /var/lib/postgresql/data, /etc/linkding/data). 'cache': data that survives restart but is regenerable on demand (thumbnails, search index, geoip db) — survives restart but EXCLUDED from snapshots, freshly initialized on the cloud side after fork. 'ephemeral': tmpfs / scratch, does not survive restart (e.g. /var/log/clickhouse-server). Cross-field rule (enforced by validator, not JSON Schema): backup is REQUIRED when role=state, FORBIDDEN otherwise.

init_chown.description: On first creation only, set ownership of the volume root to UID:GID (recursive). Needed when the container runs as a non-root UID that won't have permission on a fresh empty volume (e.g. postgres = 999:999). Skipped on subsequent runs (detected by a sentinel file '.grove-init' grove writes after the initial chown). UID and GID must both be >= 1 — assigning 0:0 (root) is rejected to prevent footguns where a host bind mount becomes root-owned and unrecoverable by the user. Despite the field name suggesting chmod, this is genuinely a chown operation; the field is named init_chown to match its actual behavior.

backup.description: How to snapshot the volume for deploy (fork to cloud) and for periodic backups. REQUIRED when volume role=state (the default); FORBIDDEN when role=cache or role=ephemeral. Cross-field rule enforced by validator.

post_restore.description: Runs once, inside this service's container, after a deploy-snapshot is restored into a fresh instance (the cloud side of a fork-deploy). Use to rewrite state that's tied to the old instance's identity — most commonly URL search-replace for apps that store absolute URLs in their database (Ghost: posts.html; Plausible: site URLs; Nextcloud: trusted_domains). ${grove.old_public_url} and ${grove.public_url} are both available here.

pre_upgrade.description: Runs inside this service's container BEFORE the image is swapped to a newer version. Use for graceful drain (flush queues, finish in-flight writes), or pre-flight backup. The OLD image is still running when this fires.

post_upgrade.description: Runs inside this service's container AFTER the new image is up and healthy. Use for one-shot post-upgrade work — DB migrations, cache rebuilds, search reindex. Failure here aborts the upgrade and grove rolls back.

cmd.description: Exec form. First element is the binary; rest are args. Runs inside the service's container with the service's env (including ${secret.X} and ${grove.X} already resolved). Wrap in ["sh", "-c", "..."] only if you need shell features (pipes, redirects).

timeout_seconds.description: Max wall-clock time the hook may run before grove kills it and treats as failure. Default 5 minutes — most migrations / search-replace fit. Long DB migrations on large fork-restores may need higher.

oneOf.description: tar.zst of the volume contents. Container is briefly stopped during snapshot for consistency.

password_secret.description: Name of secret in top-level secrets[] holding the postgres password.

oneOf.description: Online pg_dump via docker exec; output stored alongside the snapshot. Container stays running.
Uses the upstream clickhouse-backup tool. ClickHouse-specific because filesystem tar can corrupt MergeTree parts.

snapshot.description: Exec form. Run inside the volume's owning service container. MUST write its output to artifact_path. ${secret.X} and ${grove.X} interpolation available.

restore.description: Exec form. Run inside the volume's owning service container after grove places the snapshot file at artifact_path. ${secret.X} and ${grove.X} interpolation available.

artifact_path.description: Absolute path inside the container where snapshot writes the output file and restore reads it back. Grove ships the bytes between local and cloud.

quiesce_services.description: Names of sibling services in this app that grove SIGSTOPs (pauses) for the duration of the snapshot+restore, to prevent inflight writes from corrupting the dump. Use sparingly — pausing the app service produces user-visible downtime.

oneOf.description: Escape hatch for databases without first-class drivers (mysql, mongo, redis, neo4j, etc.). Author supplies snapshot+restore commands that operate on artifact_path. Prefer first-class drivers (filesystem/pg_dump/clickhouse-backup) when available — they share grove's progress reporting, error mapping, and integrity verification.

container.description: Port number the service listens on inside the container.

public.description: When true, this port is exposed to the user (local: port-forwarded to host; cloud: routed by reverse proxy). At least one service in services[] must have one port with public: true.

type.description: Discriminator. 'cmd' = exec form (run a command inside the container, exit 0 = healthy). 'http' = HTTP probe (GET path, 2xx = healthy). Explicit tag so validators can produce 'missing required cmd for type=cmd' instead of oneOf's unhelpful 'did not match exactly one schema'.

cmd.description: Exec form (type=cmd): command runs inside the container; exit 0 = healthy. Wrap in ["sh", "-c", "..."] only when shell features are needed.

port_name.description: Which port from this service's ports[] to probe. Default = the first port. Required when the service exposes multiple ports.

interval_seconds.description: How often grove runs the probe once the container has passed start_period.

timeout_seconds.description: Per-probe timeout; exceeding it counts as a failure.

start_period_seconds.description: Grace period after container start during which probe failures don't count toward unhealthy state.

category.description: Browseable category in the grove UI. Keep this enum tight — open vocabulary degrades catalog search by app #50 (per OSS catalog patterns).

url_path.description: Path on the public port grove opens in a browser after first start (e.g. "/register", "/setup").

extra_env.description: Env vars set only during CI smoke test (e.g. I_REALLY_WANT_VOLATILE_STORAGE=true for vaultwarden's missing-volume guard).

skip.description: When true, Layer 3 boot test is skipped. Use sparingly — document why in a comment in the manifest.
